Air Freight
links.description: The Air Freight data set is a ray-traced image sequence along with ground truth segmentation based on textural characteristics. (455 images + GT, each 160x120 pixels). (Formats: PNG)

links.title: Amsterdam Library of Object Images
links.description: ALOI is a color image collection of one-thousand small objects, recorded for scientific purposes. In order to capture the sensory variation in object recordings, we systematically varied viewing angle, illumination angle, and illumination color for each object, and additionally captured wide-baseline stereo images. We recorded over a hundred images of each object, yielding a total of 110,250 images for the collection. (Formats: png)

links.title: Annotated face, hand, cardiac & meat images
links.description: Most images & annotations are supplemented by various ASM/AAM analyses using the AAM-API. (Formats: bmp,asf)

links.title: Brown University Stimuli
links.description: A variety of datasets including geons, objects, and "greebles". Good for testing recognition algorithms. (Formats: pict)

links.title: CAVIAR video sequences of mall and public space behavior
links.description: 90K video frames in 90 sequences of various human activities, with XML ground truth of detection and behavior classification (Formats: MPEG2 & JPEG)

links.title: CCITT Fax standard images
links.description: 8 images (Formats: gif)

links.title: CMU PIE Database
links.description: A database of 41,368 face images of 68 people captured under 13 poses, 43 illuminations conditions, and with 4 different expressions.

links.title: CMU VASC Image Database
links.description: Images, sequences, stereo pairs (thousands of images) (Formats: Sun Rasterimage)

links.title: Caltech Image Database
links.description: about 20 images - mostly top-down views of small objects and toys. (Formats: GIF)

links.title: Columbia-Utrecht Reflectance and Texture Database
links.description: Texture and reflectance measurements for over 60 samples of 3D texture, observed with over 200 different combinations of viewing and illumination directions. (Formats: bmp)

links.title: Computational Colour Constancy Data
links.description: A dataset oriented towards computational color constancy, but useful for computer vision in general. It includes synthetic data, camera sensor data, and over 700 images. (Formats: tiff)

links.title: Content-based image retrieval database
links.description: 11 sets of color images for testing algorithms for content-based retrieval. Most sets have a description file with names of objects in each image. (Formats: jpg)

links.title: Densely Sampled View Spheres
links.description: Densely sampled view spheres - upper half of the view sphere of two toy objects with 2500 images each. (Formats: tiff)

links.title: Digital Embryos
links.description: Digital embryos are novel objects which may be used to develop and test object recognition systems. They have an organic appearance. (Formats: various formats are available on request)

links.title: El Salvador Atlas of Gastrointestinal VideoEndoscopy
links.description: Images and Videos of his-res of studies taken from Gastrointestinal Video endoscopy. (Formats: jpg, mpg, gif)

links.title: FG-NET Facial Aging Database
links.description: Database contains 1002 face images showing subjects at different ages. (Formats: jpg)

links.title: FVC2000 Fingerprint Databases
links.description: FVC2000 is the First International Competition for Fingerprint Verification Algorithms. Four fingerprint databases constitute the FVC2000 benchmark (3520 fingerprints in all).

links.title: Biometric Systems Lab
links.description: University of Bologna

links.title: Face and Gesture images and image sequences
links.description: Several image datasets of faces and gestures that are ground truth annotated for benchmarking

links.title: German Fingerspelling Database
links.description: The database contains 35 gestures and consists of 1400 image sequences that contain gestures of 20 different persons recorded under non-uniform daylight lighting conditions. (Formats: mpg,jpg)

links.title: Groningen Natural Image Database
links.description: 4000+ 1536x1024 (16 bit) calibrated outdoor images (Formats: homebrew)

links.title: ICG Testhouse sequence
links.description: 2 turntable sequences from different viewing heights, 36 images each, resolution 1000x750, color (Formats: PPM)

links.title: Institute of Computer Graphics and Vision
IEN Image Library
links.description: 1000+ images, mostly outdoor sequences (Formats: raw, ppm)

links.title: INRIA's Syntim images database
links.description: 15 color image of simple objects (Formats: gif)

links.title: INRIA's Syntim stereo databases
links.description: 34 calibrated color stereo pairs (Formats: gif)

links.title: Image Analysis Laboratory
links.description: Images obtained from a variety of imaging modalities -- raw CFA images, range images and a host of "medical images". (Formats: homebrew)

links.title: Image Database
links.description: An image database including some textures

links.title: JAFFE Facial Expression Image Database
links.description: The JAFFE database consists of 213 images of Japanese female subjects posing 6 basic facial expressions as well as a neutral pose. Ratings on emotion adjectives are also available, free of charge, for research purposes. (Formats: TIFF Grayscale images.)

links.title: MIT Vision Texture
links.description: Image archive (100+ images) (Formats: ppm)

links.title: Machine Vision
links.description: Images from the textbook by Jain, Kasturi, Schunck (20+ images) (Formats: GIF TIFF)

links.title: Mammography Image Databases
links.description: 100 or more images of mammograms with ground truth. Additional images available by request, and links to several other mammography databases are provided. (Formats: homebrew)

links.title: Middlebury Stereo Data Sets with Ground Truth
links.description: Six multi-frame stereo data sets of scenes containing planar regions. Each data set contains 9 color images and subpixel-accuracy ground-truth data. (Formats: ppm)

links.title: Middlebury Stereo Vision Research Page
links.description: Middlebury College

links.title: Modis Airborne simulator, Gallery and data set
links.description: High Altitude Imagery from around the world for environmental modeling in support of NASA EOS program (Formats: JPG and HDF)

links.title: NLM HyperDoc Visible Human Project
links.description: Color, CAT and MRI image samples - over 30 images (Formats: jpeg)

links.title: National Design Repository
links.description: Over 55,000 3D CAD and solid models of (mostly) mechanical/machined engineering designs. (Formats: gif,vrml,wrl,stp,sat)

links.title: OSU (MSU) 3D Object Model Database
links.description: several sets of 3D object models collected over several years to use in object recognition research (Formats: homebrew, vrml)

links.title: OSU (MSU/WSU) Range Image Database
links.description: Hundreds of real and synthetic images (Formats: gif, homebrew)

links.title: OSU/SAMPL Database: Range Images, 3D Models, Stills, Motion Sequences
links.description: Over 1000 range images, 3D object models, still images and motion sequences (Formats: gif, ppm, vrml, homebrew)

links.title: Signal Analysis and Machine Perception Laboratory
Otago Optical Flow Evaluation Sequences
links.description: Synthetic and real sequences with machine-readable ground truth optical flow fields, plus tools to generate ground truth for new sequences. (Formats: ppm,tif,homebrew)

links.title: Photometric 3D Surface Texture Database
links.description: This is the first 3D texture database which provides both full real surface rotations and registered photometric stereo data (30 textures, 1680 images). (Formats: TIFF)

links.title: SEQUENCES FOR OPTICAL FLOW ANALYSIS (SOFA)
links.description: 9 synthetic sequences designed for testing motion analysis applications, including full ground truth of motion and camera parameters. (Formats: gif)

links.title: Sequences for Flow Based Reconstruction
links.description: synthetic sequence for testing structure from motion algorithms (Formats: pgm)

links.title: Stereo Images with Ground Truth Disparity and Occlusion
links.description: a small set of synthetic images of a hallway with varying amounts of noise added. Use these images to benchmark your stereo algorithm. (Formats: raw, viff (khoros), or tiff)

links.title: Stuttgart Range Image Database
links.description: A collection of synthetic range images taken from high-resolution polygonal models available on the web (Formats: homebrew)

links.title: The AR Face Database
links.description: Contains over 4,000 color images corresponding to 126 people's faces (70 men and 56 women). Frontal views with variations in facial expressions, illumination, and occlusions. (Formats: RAW (RGB 24-bit))

links.title: The MIT-CSAIL Database of Objects and Scenes
links.description: Database for testing multiclass object detection and scene recognition algorithms. Over 72,000 images with 2873 annotated frames. More than 50 annotated object classes. (Formats: jpg)

links.title: The RVL SPEC-DB (SPECularity DataBase)
links.description: A collection of over 300 real images of 100 objects taken under three different illuminaiton conditions (Diffuse/Ambient/Directed). -- Use these images to test algorithms for detecting and compensating specular highlights in color images. (Formats: TIFF )

links.title: The Xm2vts database
links.description: The XM2VTSDB contains four digital recordings of 295 people taken over a period of four months. This database contains both image and video data of faces.

links.title: Centre for Vision, Speech and Signal Processing
Traffic Image Sequences and 'Marbled Block' Sequence
links.description: thousands of frames of digitized traffic image sequences as well as the 'Marbled Block' sequence (grayscale images) (Formats: GIF)

links.title: U Oulu wood and knots database
links.description: Includes classifications - 1000+ color images (Formats: ppm)

links.title: UCID - an Uncompressed Colour Image Database
links.description: a benchmark database for image retrieval with predefined ground truth. (Formats: tiff)

links.title: UMass Vision Image Archive
links.description: Large image database with aerial, space, stereo, medical images and more. (Formats: homebrew)

links.title: USF Range Image Data with Segmentation Ground Truth
links.description: 80 image sets (Formats: Sun rasterimage)

links.title: University of Oulu Physics-based Face Database
links.description: contains color images of faces under different illuminants and camera calibration conditions as well as skin spectral reflectance measurements of each person.

links.title: Machine Vision and Media Processing Unit
University of Oulu Texture Database
links.description: Database of 320 surface textures, each captured under three illuminants, six spatial resolutions and nine rotation angles. A set of test suites is also provided so that texture segmentation, classification, and retrieval algorithms can be tested in a standard manner. (Formats: bmp, ras, xv)

links.title: View Sphere Database
links.description: Images of 8 objects seen from many different view points. The view sphere is sampled using a geodesic with 172 images/sphere. Two sets for training and testing are available. (Formats: ppm)

links.title: Wiry Object Recognition Database
links.description: Thousands of images of a cart, ladder, stool, bicycle, chairs, and cluttered scenes with ground truth labelings of edges and regions. (Formats: jpg)

links.title: Yale Face Database
links.description: 165 images (15 individuals) with different lighting, expression, and occlusion configurations.

links.title: Yale Face Database B
links.description: 5760 single light source images of 10 subjects each seen under 576 viewing conditions (9 poses x 64 illumination conditions). (Formats: PGM)

links.title: Center for Computational Vision and Control
DeepMind QA Corpus
links.description: Textual QA corpus from CNN and DailyMail. More than 300K documents in total. [Paper](http://arxiv.org/abs/1506.03340) for reference.

links.title: YouTube-8M Dataset
links.description: YouTube-8M is a large-scale labeled video dataset that consists of 8 million YouTube video IDs and associated labels from a diverse vocabulary of 4800 visual entities.

links.title: Open Images dataset
links.description: Open Images is a dataset of ~9 million URLs to images that have been annotated with labels spanning over 6000 categories.

links.title: Visual Object Classes Challenge 2012 (VOC2012)
links.description: VOC2012 dataset containing 12k images with 20 annotated classes for object detection and segmentation.

links.title: Fashion-MNIST
links.description: MNIST like fashion product dataset consisting of a training set of 60,000 examples and a test set of 10,000 examples. Each example is a 28x28 grayscale image, associated with a label from 10 classes.

links.title: Large-scale Fashion (DeepFashion) Database
links.description: Contains over 800,000 diverse fashion images.  Each image in this dataset is labeled with 50 categories, 1,000 descriptive attributes, bounding box and clothing landmarks

links.title: FakeNewsCorpus
links.description: Contains about 10 million news articles classified using [opensources.co](http://opensources.co) types

links.title: LLVIP
links.description: 15488 visible-infrared paired images (30976 images) for low-light vision research, [Project_Page](https://bupt-ai-cz.github.io/LLVIP/)

links.title: MSDA
links.description: Over over 5 million images from 5 different domains for multi-source ocr/text recognition DA research, [Project_Page](https://bupt-ai-cz.github.io/Meta-SelfLearning/)

links.title: SANAD: Single-Label Arabic News Articles Dataset for Automatic Text Categorization
links.description: SANAD Dataset is a large collection of Arabic news articles that can be used in different Arabic NLP tasks such as Text Classification and Word Embedding. The articles were collected using Python scripts written specifically for three popular news websites: AlKhaleej, AlArabiya and Akhbarona.

links.title: Referit3D
links.description: Two large-scale and complementary visio-linguistic datasets (aka Nr3D and Sr3D) for identifying fine-grained 3D objects in ScanNet scenes. Nr3D contains 41.5K natural, free-form utterances, and Sr3d contains 83.5K template-based utterances.

links.title: SQuAD
links.description: Stanford released ~100,000 English QA pairs and ~50,000 unanswerable questions

links.title: FQuAD
links.description: ~25,000 French QA pairs released by Illuin Technology

links.title: GermanQuAD and GermanDPR
links.description: deepset released ~14,000 German QA pairs

links.title: SberQuAD
links.description: Sberbank released ~90,000 Russian QA pairs

links.title: ArtEmis
links.description: Contains 450K affective annotations of emotional responses and linguistic explanations for 80,000 artworks of WikiArt.

TensorForce - A TensorFlow library for applied reinforcement learning
Trax — Deep Learning with Clear Code and Speed
Flax - a neural network ecosystem for JAX that is designed for flexibility
haystack: an open-source neural search framework
InsNet - A neural network library for building instance-dependent NLP models with padding-free dynamic batching
Nebullvm
links.description: Easy-to-use library to boost deep learning inference leveraging multiple deep learning compilers.

links.description: TensorFlow's Visualization Toolkit

links.title: Neptune
links.description: Lightweight tool for experiment tracking and results visualization.

links.title: DAGsHub
links.description: Community platform for Open Source ML – Manage experiments, data & models and create collaborative ML projects easily.

links.title: DVC
links.description: DVC is built to make ML models shareable and reproducible. It is designed to handle large files, data sets, machine learning models, and metrics as well as code.

links.title: CML
links.description: CML helps you bring your favorite DevOps tools to machine learning.

links.title: MLEM
links.description: MLEM is a tool to easily package, deploy and serve Machine Learning models. It seamlessly supports a variety of scenarios like real-time serving and batch processing.
